title: Richard Taylor still hopes for Live Action Evangelion
display_title: Richard Taylor still hopes for Live Action Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Richard Taylor still hopes for Live Action Evangelion             Quote Post by V » Tue May 13, 2008 9:45 pm http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=syDws-bNaH0 Fanvid of Weta's Richard Taylor at Wellington New Zealand's pop culture con. Keep the dream alive!

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue May 13, 2008 9:58 pm well, Speed Racer sucked major behind at the box office (as I predicted) let's see how the Transformers sequel and the live action Akira, Robotech and Dragon Ball all do

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Wed May 14, 2008 2:04 am THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: well, Speed Racer sucked major behind at the box office (as I predicted) let's see how the Transformers sequel and the live action Akira, Robotech and Dragon Ball all do

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Wed May 14, 2008 1:09 pm I saw Speed Racer last weekend. From a visual perspective, it was actually really good, although I think the digital projection helped a lot. The plot was about as good as you can get out of something like Speed Racer. Probably the closest thing to live-action anime that's ever been made. There's still potential out there for an Eva movie, I think -- just don't let the Wachowski Bros. handle it, or they'll add ninjas.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Wed May 14, 2008 3:53 pm if you think about it its not just 1 movie. and if the first 1 is an epic failure than there wont be any more. I think a Live Action tv show would be more realistic. When they sat down to make the Live action eva movie werent they overbudget in like the first 30 mins?

[]:             Quote Post by Super Chris » Wed May 14, 2008 9:09 pm Zeak wrote: THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: well, Speed Racer sucked major behind at the box office (as I predicted) let's see how the Transformers sequel and the live action Akira, Robotech and Dragon Ball all do

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed May 14, 2008 9:34 pm Why exactly to people have this guy sign a DVD of a film he had absolutely nothing to do with the process of making or distributing?

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Wed May 14, 2008 9:48 pm Super Chris wrote: As far as Evangelion, unfortunately the one thing that is at the top of my list of things that would screw it up <snip> That would be, rather than allowing third impact to happen, they manage to avert it at the last moment.

[]:             Quote Post by Super Chris » Wed May 14, 2008 10:28 pm I think I may be the only person who's worried about it, but it seems like a stereotypically american thing they'd do, since we generally like stories where the world gets saved, whereas in Evangelion the world ends. But anyway, even if I'm overestimating how likely it is they would do that, it still tops my list of 'things that would screw Evangelion up'.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed May 14, 2008 10:49 pm drinian wrote: I saw Speed Racer last weekend. From a visual perspective, it was actually really good, although I think the digital projection helped a lot.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Wed May 14, 2008 11:12 pm The colors in the trailers for Speed Racer made me think the crew working on it was taking speed.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Thu May 15, 2008 1:40 am Still Live Action Eva if you think about it you cant realy stuff it up because at the end of the day you can always go back and watch the original or even rebuild for that matter. You wont be at a loss no matter how it turns out. Still thay cant take it away from Japan as most of the parts are vital to be in Japan. For starters Tokyo-3 I doubt it will be Washington-3 or NewYork-3. Bes bet not to warry about it. And look forward to a huge jump in Evangelion fans and Merchandise.

[]:             Quote Post by Zerogoki » Thu May 15, 2008 9:40 am drinian wrote: There's still potential out there for an Eva movie, I think -- just don't let the Wachowski Bros. handle it, or they'll add ninjas.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Thu May 15, 2008 1:05 pm yeah, reminds of when Martin Scorcese went against type and did New York, New York and how much of a flop that turned out to be

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu May 15, 2008 3:55 pm Zerogoki wrote: Before Speed Racer I thought they'd be ideal for Live-Eva, seeing as they did such a good job of handling the seriousness of V for Vendetta. Not so sure now though...

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Fri May 16, 2008 5:48 am Did it even have a story behind it?

[]:             Quote Post by Zerogoki » Fri May 16, 2008 3:15 pm NAveryW wrote: Keep in mind that Speed Racer 's source material was never that serious...

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sat May 17, 2008 8:20 am That's because there was nothing to screw up. The series itself had very little story line. Speed races against the odds and then he wins. That's your whole story right there basically. They could do anything they wanted to as long as they kept that concept and the original cast intact. Plus the only fan base is people with nostalgic memories at best. It's not like the series had a huge following over here. It was just another Saturday morning cartoon along with the Herculoids and Thundarr the Barbarian. Actually it was probably one of the safest anime to do because most people in the US don't even know it's an anime. It can be just a regular movie. Still it is not doing as well as it was supposed to. If the Akira and DragonBallZ movies don't do well either that may be the end of anime to live action in Hollywood.

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelion217 » Mon May 26, 2008 9:11 pm well, Speed Racer sucked major behind at the box office (as I predicted)

[]:             Quote Post by Typhon » Tue May 27, 2008 11:27 pm Super Chris wrote: Zeak wrote: THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: well, Speed Racer sucked major behind at the box office (as I predicted) let's see how the Transformers sequel and the live action Akira, Robotech and Dragon Ball all do

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue May 13, 2008 9:58 pm well, Speed Racer sucked major behind at the
